title: acrostichum urvillei
intro: Acrostichum urvillei, also known as the Australian leathery fern, is a species of fern native to Australia and New Guinea. It is traditionally used in medicine due to its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and alleviating respiratory issues. Acrostichum urvillei is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
